Evolve Church · Milwaukee, WI
What We Stand On
Our Beliefs

These convictions shape how we worship, how we serve, and how we love our city.

At Evolve, we believe the church should grow to meet every generation while staying rooted in timeless truth. Our beliefs aren't a checklist, they're the foundation for a life fully alive in Christ.

01 — Foundation

What We Believe

The foundational convictions that anchor everything at Evolve Church

01 · Scripture

The Bible

The Bible is God's word to humanity, written by human authors under the supernatural guidance of the Holy Spirit. It is truth without any mixture of error and completely relevant to our daily lives.

2 Timothy 3:15–17 · Hebrews 4:12
02 · Doctrine

The Trinity

God has existed in relationship with Himself for all eternity — one substance in three persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. Each member possesses equal power and authority.

Matthew 28:19 · 1 John 5:7
03 · God

The Father

God is all-powerful, all-knowing, and ever-present. He is our Father — loving, compassionate, and eternally faithful to His people and His promises. In Him we live, move, and exist.

John 3:16 · Psalm 11:4–6
04 · Christ

Jesus Christ

Jesus is completely human and completely God. He lived a perfect life, died as our substitute, and rose from the dead — making it possible for all of us to be restored to God.

John 1:1,14 · Romans 3:23–26
05 · Spirit

Holy Spirit

The Holy Spirit guides believers into truth, convicts people of sin, comforts us, and gives spiritual gifts. Evidence of His presence is seen in the fruit He produces in our lives.

John 16:7–14 · Galatians 5:22–23
06 · Grace

Salvation

Salvation is a gift of grace — not earned through our own efforts, but received through faith in Christ's death and resurrection. The blood of Jesus is the only way of forgiveness.

Ephesians 2:8–10 · Romans 10:9–10
07 · Community

The Church

The Church is a local community of baptized believers unified through faith in Christ — working together in love and unity to bring the Gospel to the world and glorify Christ.

Matthew 16:18 · Ephesians 1:22–23
08 · Connection

Prayer

Prayer is our direct line of communication with God. Through Jesus, we can come to the Father personally. No meaningful work happens without prayer preceding and continuing through it.

Philippians 4:6 · 1 Thessalonians 5:17
09 · Obedience

Baptism

Baptism is a symbolic act to publicly declare one's salvation. We practice full-immersion baptism, symbolizing the death and resurrection that Jesus went through for us.

Matthew 28:19 · Acts 2:38
10 · Remembrance

Communion

Communion is a sacred time when the bread and cup express the body and blood of Christ. Believers take communion in remembrance of Christ's sacrifice on the cross.

1 Corinthians 11:23–25 · Matthew 26:26–28
11 · Power

Divine Healing

We believe God's supernatural power to heal is still for today. Through the power of the Holy Spirit and prayer, Jesus can heal our earthly bodies and restore wholeness.

Isaiah 53:4–5 · James 5:14–17
12 · Hope

Eternity

Man was created to exist forever — either eternally separated from God by sin, or in eternal union with God through salvation. Heaven and Hell are real places of eternal existence.

John 3:16 · Revelation 20:12–15
Our Mission

Love God.
Love People.
Love Ourselves.

Everything we do flows from these three life-giving commitments.

Our Vision

Worship.
Word.
Works.

Three pillars that guide how we engage with God and one another.

Our Passion

Reaching
the Lost.
Serving Our City.

Our actions are proof of God's love — we show what we believe.

02 — Deeper

More of What We Believe

Expanding on the foundations of our faith

Man is made in the image of God — the supreme object of His creation. Every human personality is uniquely created, possesses dignity, and is worthy of respect and Christian love. Man became separated from God through sinful disobedience, and his only hope of redemption is in Jesus Christ.

Repentance is a commitment to turn away from sin in every area of life and follow Christ. Through repentance we individually receive forgiveness of sins and appropriate salvation.

Genesis 1:26–30 · Acts 2:38 · Romans 5:12–21

The five-fold ministry positions of apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers are gifts given to each local church and the universal church. These gifts are for equipping believers to spread the gospel. We believe both men and women are sovereignly chosen by God for these ministry positions.

Ephesians 4:8–13 · 1 Corinthians 12:4–30

We believe that as part of Christ's work of salvation, it is the Father's will for believers to become whole, healthy, and successful in all areas of life which includes: spiritually, mentally and emotionally, physically, and financially. God desires His people to flourish in every dimension.

Isaiah 26:3 · Deuteronomy 28:1–14 · 3 John 1:2

Jesus Christ will physically and visibly return to earth for the second time to establish His authority. This will occur at a time undisclosed by scripture. So we live every day in expectation of Christ's return.

Matthew 24:30 · 1 Thessalonians 4:15–17 · Revelation 1:7
03 — Culture

Our Culture Code

The irrefutable principles that guide how we live, lead, and serve

Evolve

We believe the church should evolve to meet the needs of every generation, culture, and context.

Love

We love first, because God is love.

Faith

We have faith in God, faith in people, and faith for miracles.

T.R.U.

We are transparent, real, and unedited. This is the best way to walk in integrity.

Generosity

We give just to give, not to get.

Service

We use our hands to show our hearts.

Worship

We use our lives to express God's worth. We fully engage to show appreciation to God.

Excellence

We hold ourselves to a high standard in our words, actions, and appearance.

Discipline

We are committed to training, structure, and lifestyles that generate growth.

Relationship

We belong together. We love together. We serve together.

Honor

We honor God, value people, and add value to everyone around us.

Fun

We enjoy every moment. God wants laughter.

Take a Next Step

You Belong Here

Whether you're new to faith or ready to go deeper, there's a place for you at Evolve. We'd love to connect with you.
